function.arguments: {"requirements":[{"title":"Greeting endpoint returns JSON response","description":"The /hello endpoint must return a JSON object with a 'message' field containing a greeting string. The response must have Content-Type application/json and return HTTP 200.","acceptance_criteria":["the behavior specified by \"Greeting endpoint returns JSON response\" is implemented and returns the described result","an automated test asserts \"Greeting endpoint returns JSON response\""]},{"title":"Goodbye endpoint returns JSON response","description":"The /goodbye endpoint must return a JSON object with a 'message' field containing a farewell string, following the same pattern as /hello.","acceptance_criteria":["the behavior specified by \"Goodbye endpoint returns JSON response\" is implemented and returns the described result","an automated test asserts \"Goodbye endpoint returns JSON response\""]},{"title":"UI displays messages from both endpoints","description":"The JavaScript frontend must fetch from both /hello and /goodbye endpoints and display the returned messages in the HTML page.","acceptance_criteria":["the behavior specified by \"UI displays messages from both endpoints\" is implemented and returns the described result","an automated test asserts \"UI displays messages from both endpoints\""]}]}
